reasoning_trace: Analyzed stomach pain reports in S01, S04, S07, and S09. Found a 100% correlation with dinners eaten after 11:00 PM during work deadlines.
Examined headache reports in S02, S06, and S08. Noticed a consistent link with low water intake (2-3 glasses) and high-pressure work days.

Investigated the 'headaches mostly gone' comment in S03. This suggests that when the user isn't in a 'busy at work' phase or is potentially hydrating better, the symptom subsides, reinforcing the dehydration link.
Identified a hierarchical root cause: 'Work Deadlines/Sprints'. This single factor drives the late eating (stomach pain), the neglect of hydration (headaches), and the prolonged sitting (back pain).
Evaluated the lower back pain in S03. While only explicitly reported once, the lifestyle factor (10 hours sitting) is a recurring theme in 'long work sessions' mentioned in S01, S02, S04, and S06.

Checked for delayed onset: Fatigue in S05 appears after several weeks of reported high-intensity work and late nights, suggesting a cumulative sleep debt and nutritional depletion from 'eating out every day'.
Rejected 'random stomach condition' hypothesis mentioned by user in S09 because the temporal link to late eating is too consistent across 4 separate months.

patterns.pattern_title: Post-Prandial Gastric Distress from Late-Night Eating

supporting_evidence.note: Recurrence of burning pain after 11:00pm dinner.
Third instance following a midnight dinner.

patterns.temporal_logic: Symptoms consistently appear within 1-12 hours following a meal consumed after 11:00 PM (S01, S04, S07, S09).
patterns.causal_mechanism: Eating late at night, especially under stress, increases gastric acid secretion. Lying down shortly after a late meal can cause acid reflux and irritation of the gastric lining (gastritis-like symptoms).
patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern has four distinct occurrences with zero counter-instances where the trigger occurred without the symptom.

supporting_evidence.note: Afternoon headaches with only 2 glasses of water and all-day screen time.
Headaches return during a big project week with 3 glasses of water and high caffeine intake.
Headache during massive release deadline with 'barely any water'.

counter_evidence.note: Headaches 'mostly gone' when the user was not reporting a high-pressure deadline.

patterns.temporal_logic: Headaches occur in the afternoon (3-4pm) following a full morning/early afternoon of low fluid intake and high screen exposure (S02, S06).
patterns.causal_mechanism: Dehydration leads to reduced blood volume and brain tissue shrinkage, triggering pain receptors. This is exacerbated by digital eye strain and muscle tension from prolonged screen use.
patterns.confidence_reasoning: Strong correlation across three months, with a clear biological mechanism and positive response when the trigger is absent.

supporting_evidence.note: Work deadline leads to late dinner and stomach pain.
Big project leads to low water, high caffeine, and headaches.
Massive release deadline leads to dehydration and headaches.

patterns.temporal_logic: The 'Work Deadline' or 'Project' context precedes all reported lifestyle failures (late eating, low water, sedentary behavior) across the entire 11-week timeline.
patterns.causal_mechanism: Occupational stress and high workload lead to 'time scarcity,' causing the user to prioritize work over basic physiological needs (hydration, timely nutrition, sleep), resulting in multi-system symptoms.
patterns.confidence_reasoning: This root cause explains the clustering of all other symptoms and the recurring nature of the episodes.

supporting_evidence.note: Lower back pain after sitting for 10 hours straight with no exercise.

patterns.temporal_logic: Symptom reported specifically after a period of 10-hour sedentary sessions (S03).
patterns.causal_mechanism: Prolonged sitting causes compression of the lumbar discs and weakening of the core muscles, leading to mechanical back pain.
patterns.confidence_reasoning: The biological link is clear, but it is only explicitly reported as a primary concern in one session.
